diagnosis.summary: The deterministic Office pre-parse was invoked directly through createBashExecutionTool().invoke() during agent initialization. The acceptance request did not reach a corresponding CodeAPI /exec before the empty assistant row was persisted, so the request stalled in the tool scheduling or initialization layer rather than in Excel parsing.
evidence: The current-turn file upload reached CodeAPI at 2026-07-19T14:22:47Z.
No attributable CodeAPI /exec followed for the vip998 request before the assistant row was saved.
Mongo persisted an empty assistant row 213.861 seconds after the user message with unfinished=false and error=false.
The direct pre-parse invoke had no bounded timeout and was not wired to the request AbortSignal.
The existing semantic-empty guard runs after model completion and therefore cannot protect a request stalled before completion.

decision: Do not redeploy this artifact. A replacement design must use a bounded, abort-aware pre-parse operation and cover pre-model early-abort persistence with an integration test.
